title: Carmakers Defeat Most Allegations in UK Dieselgate Class Action
summary: Car manufacturers including Mercedes-Benz Group AG, Ford Motor Co. and Renault SA largely won a case over allegations they manipulated vehicles to cheat on emissions tests, after a London court rejected most of the claims in the closely-watched class action case.

title: Miner ERG Weighs Breakup to Split Assets Between Its Owners
summary: The owners of Eurasian Resources Group are considering splitting the mining group into two businesses, in an overhaul that would separate its Kazakh and international operations.

title: Delayed Xbox Layoffs Leave Employees Scared And Nervous
summary: Xbox cut 1,600 jobs this week and plans to cut another 1,600 over the next 12 months, leaving remaining staff on tenterhooks

title: 'Moana,' Rolling Stones, Retail Spending: Look Ahead
summary: Bloomberg's Lisa Mateo joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss the new released live action Moana movie, the new Rolling Stones album "Foreign Tongues," June CPI, PPI, and retail sails. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: S&P 500 Rises Before Earnings as SK Hynix Debuts: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders drove stocks higher in the countdown to the earnings season, with SK Hynix Inc. jumping in its debut and the market parsing the latest geopolitical developments.

title: Youth Sports Cost Debate Rekindled After US Soccer Exit
summary: Bloomberg's Randall Williams joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss the growing cost of youth sports for American families, which has become a $40 billion-a-year private industry. The high cost is listed as a factor in why US Soccer can't take the next step on the world stage. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Heads for Weekly Loss as Traders Consider Iran, Rates Path
summary: Gold headed for a weekly loss as investors assessed renewed fighting in the Middle East and the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

title: Households All-In On Equities: Cameron Dawson
summary:
Cameron Dawson, CEO of NewEdge Wealth, joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss financial planning and allocating personal portfolios.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ING Weighs Venezuela Commodity Deals as Client Requests Rise
summary: ING Groep, one of the top financiers of commodity trading, is fielding “a lot” of calls from clients to support Venezuelan natural resource deals as the country revamps exports after years of US sanctions, its new Americas commodity finance head said.

title: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Over But Talks on Deal to Continue
summary:
President Donald Trump said the US would continue talks with Iran but considers the ceasefire between the countries to be over.
“The Islamic Republic of Iran has asked us to continue ‘talks.’ We have agreed to do so, but the United States has stated to them, in no uncertain terms, that the Cease Fire is OVER!,” Trump said Friday on social media. Trita Parsi, Executive Vice President at the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft joined to discuss also saying the track record of the US in the region, particularly in terms of democratizing countries, is abysmal. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Eases as US, Iran Continue Talks After Renewed Fighting
summary: Oil prices slipped as the US and Iran appear set to continue talks following a flare-up in attacks that strained their ceasefire and deterred tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: More Americans Are Getting Prenups, Even If They Aren't Rich
summary: Bloomberg's Nikki Waller joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Belle Burden’s blockbuster memoir, 'Strangers,' has riveted readers with the tale of her marriage’s sudden implosion and aftermath. They also discuss the rising popularity of prenups has been driven by changing financial dynamics of marriage. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Exxon Supports SEC Proposal to End Mandatory Quarterly Reports
summary: ExxonMobil Holdings Corp. supports a US Securities and Exchange Commission proposal to replace quarterly financial reporting with semiannual disclosures, bucking the trend among most respondents the regulator’s request for comments.

title: Prediction Market Bets Surge on World Cup Mania
summary: Rabid interest in the World Cup has helped to drive more than $3 billion of wagers on a prediction market backed by Robinhood Markets Inc. and Susquehanna International Group, which launched just a few months ago.

title: Headline: Trust Breakdown Between Fed, Consumer Inflation: Kroszner
summary:
Randy Kroszner, University of Chicago Booth School professor of economics and former Fed Governor, joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money."
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Hynix's US Debut, Trump Accounts: Money Roundtable
summary: Bloomberg's Katie Greifeld, David Gura, and Mandeep Singh join Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." The discuss the US trading debut of SK Hynix, the rollout of Trump accounts, and the escalation seen this week in the US-Iran conflict.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aston Martin Lender Group Led by Arini, BlackRock, Sculptor
summary: Aston Martin creditors led by Arini Capital Management, BlackRock Inc. and Sculptor Capital have joined forces in an effort to stop the distressed British luxury carmaker from pursuing any debt deal they fear could erode their standing.

title: Trump Says Ceasefire With Iran Is Over
summary: President Donald Trump said the US would continue talks with Iran but considers the ceasefire between the countries to be over. James Jeffrey, former US ambassador to Iraq and Turkey, talks about Trump's objectives in Iran and why the US is targeting infrastructure. He speaks on "Balance of Power." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Won’t Sign Housing Bill But Will Allow It to Become Law
summary: President Donald Trump said he would not sign a bipartisan housing bill in protest over lawmakers declining to approve a separate voter-ID bill he has prioritized. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aluminum Declines as EGA Restarts Key Alumina Refinery in UAE
summary: Aluminum fell after Emirates Global Aluminium said it restarted a key alumina plant in Abu Dhabi that was attacked in the early stages of the Iran war.

title: Nasdaq Says SK Hynix Bonanza Opens Path to More Foreign Listings
summary: Nasdaq Inc. President Nelson Griggs said SK Hynix Inc.’s blockbuster listing is spurring other international companies to consider the US for either initial public offerings or the sales of American depositary receipts.

title: BHP Explores Sale of Chilean Desalination Plant, Power Lines
summary: BHP Group is exploring the sale of a desalination plant in Chile, along with its electricity transmission assets, extending a strategy of monetizing infrastructure as the world’s biggest miner concentrates capital on its core copper business, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Wheat Soars as Ukraine Attacks on Russia Seen as Export Risk
summary: Wheat prices jumped Friday as traders weighed potential shipping disruptions following Ukraine’s attacks on top exporter Russia.

title: SK Chairman Promises ‘Much, Much Bigger’ US Investment Plan
summary: SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won, fresh off a landmark US stock offering for the conglomerate’s memory-chip business, said he has an ambitious plan to invest more money in America.

title: Sports Bet Pays Off as Omers Reaps Nearly 60% Gain on MLSE
summary: Ontario’s pension fund for government workers generated a return of close to 60% in less than three years on a bet tied to Toronto’s major league sports teams, vindicating an investment that was initially viewed as pricey.

title: Stocks Waver as SK Hynix Makes US Debut | Open Interest 7/10/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." SK Hynix makes Wall Street history with the biggest-ever U.S. debut by a foreign company. Delta beats earnings expectations, but shares fluctuate on a cloudy outlook. And earnings season ramps up next week, with Wall Street's biggest banks set to report another blockbuster quarter. Emily Roland of Manulife John Hancock Investments, John Stoltzfus of Oppenheimer Asset Management, and Di Zhou of Thornburg Investment Management weigh in as traders hold off on making big bets ahead of the weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Traders Hedge for FX Volatility Return Amid Fed Uncertainty
summary: Currency traders are starting to buy protection against bigger exchange-rate swings after months of calm, as banks warn that shifting Federal Reserve expectations and elevated geopolitical tensions could be about to jolt markets.

title: Sports Investing Made Accessible for Everyone
summary: Marques Colston, former wide receiver for the New Orleans Saints, discusses the focus of his private sports venture 'The Champion Fund,' and his efforts to make sports investing accessible to all. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Stock Market and a “Phenomena of our Lifetimes”
summary: Something big has changed in the makeup of the US economy.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 7/10/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)
